input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Firewall`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Firewall` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: ic.5.2.3
            TITLE: a firewall must separate the fuel tank from the driver, per t.1.8

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.2.3: A Firewall must separate the Fuel Tank from the driver, per T.1.8
IC.5.2.3 A Firewall must separate the Fuel Tank from the driver, per T.1.8

RULE: t.1.8.1
            TITLE: requirement

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.1: Requirement
T.1.8.1 Requirement 
A Firewall(s) must separate the driver compartment and any portion of the Driver Harness 
from:  
a. All components of the Fuel System, the engine oil, the liquid cooling systems, any lithium 
batteries 
b. (EV only) All Tractive System components other than Outboard Wheel Motors EV.4.1.3 
where mounted at the wheels or on the front control arms

RULE: t.1.8.2
            TITLE: construction

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.2: Construction
T.1.8.2 Construction 
Any Firewall must:  
a. Be a non permeable surface made from a rigid, Nonflammable Material  
b. Seal completely against the passage of fluids (the Firewall itself, edges, any pass throughs 
and Floor Closeout) 
c. Be rigidly mounted

RULE: t.1.8.4
            TITLE: details

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.4: Details
T.1.8.4 Details 
a. Firewalls composed of multiple panels must overlap and be sealed at the joints 
Sealing between firewalls must not be a stressed part of the Firewall 
b. Grommets must be used to seal any pass through for wiring, cables, etc
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 58 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Any seals or adhesives used with the Firewall must be rated for the application 
environment

RULE: t.1.8.3
            TITLE: positioning

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.3: Positioning
T.1.8.3 Positioning  
The Firewall must extend sufficiently far upwards and/or rearwards and/or sideways where 
any point on the drivers body less than 100 mm above the bottom of the helmet of the tallest 
driver must not be in direct line of sight with any part given in T.1.8.1 above

RULE: ev.4.3.7
            TITLE: pressure relief valves must not have line of sight to the driver, with the firewall installed or

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.3.7: Pressure relief valves must not have line of sight to the driver, with the Firewall installed or
EV.4.3.7 Pressure relief valves must not have line of sight to the driver, with the Firewall installed or 
removed

RULE: t.1.9.2
            TITLE: tractive system firewalls must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.9.2: Tractive System Firewalls must be:
T.1.9.2 Tractive System Firewalls must be:  
a. Made of aluminum 
b. Grounded, refer to EV.6.7 Grounding

RULE: t.1.8.5
            TITLE: (ev only) the accumulator container must not be part of the firewall

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.5: (EV only) The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Firewall
T.1.8.5 (EV only) The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Firewall

RULE: t.1.9.1
            TITLE: tractive system firewalls must meet the requirements of t.1.8 above

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.9.1: Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above
T.1.9.1 Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above

RULE: ev.6.7.1
            TITLE: grounding is required for:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.7.1: Grounding is required for:
EV.6.7.1 Grounding is required for: 
a. Parts of the vehicle which are 100 mm or less from any Tractive System component 
b. The Tractive System Firewall T.1.9

RULE: t.1.6.2
            TITLE: insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the driver’s seat or firewall.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.2: Insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the Driver’s Seat or Firewall.
T.1.6.2 Insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the Driver’s Seat or Firewall.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 57 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.1.7.2
            TITLE: the floor closeout must extend from the foot area to the firewall

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.7.2: The Floor Closeout must extend from the foot area to the firewall
T.1.7.2 The Floor Closeout must extend from the foot area to the firewall

RULE: t.5.6.2
            TITLE: catch cans must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.6.2: Catch cans must be:
T.5.6.2 Catch cans must be: 
a. Capable of containing boiling water without deformation 
b. Located rearwards of the Firewall below the driver’s shoulder level 
c. Positively retained, using no tie wraps or tape

RULE: f.10.4.4
            TITLE: holes and openings for airflow:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.4.4: Holes and Openings for airflow:
F.10.4.4 Holes and Openings for airflow: 
a. Must be round.  Slots are prohibited 
b. Should be maximum 10 mm diameter
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 51 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Must not have line of sight to the driver, with the Firewall installed or removed

RULE: t.1.1.4
            TITLE: during this test:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.1.4: During this test:
T.1.1.4 During this test:  
a. The steering wheel, steering column, seat and all padding may be removed 
b. The shifter, shift mechanism, or clutch mechanism must not be removed unless it is 
integral with the steering wheel and is removed with the steering wheel 
c. The firewall must not be moved or removed 
d. Cables, wires, hoses, tubes, etc. must not block movement of the template 
During inspection, the steering column, for practical purposes, will not be removed.  The 
template may be maneuvered around the steering column, but not any fixed supports.   
For ease of use, the template may contain a slot at the front center that the steering column 
may pass through.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 55 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.1.6.1
            TITLE: when seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.1: When seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to
T.1.6.1 When seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to 
ensure that the driver will not contact any metal or other materials which may become heated 
to a surface temperature above 60°C.

RULE: f.5.16
            TITLE: component protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.16: Component Protection
F.5.16 Component Protection  
When specified in the rules, components must be protected by one or both of:  
a. Fully Triangulated structure with tubes meeting F.3.2.1.n  
b. Structure Equivalent to the above, as determined per F.4.1.3

RULE: d.4.1.4
            TITLE: blue flag - pull into the designated passing zone to be passed by a faster competitor. obey the

            CONTENT:
            rule D.4.1.4: Blue Flag - Pull into the designated passing zone to be passed by a faster competitor. Obey the
D.4.1.4 Blue Flag - Pull into the designated passing zone to be passed by a faster competitor. Obey the 
corner workers signals at the end of the passing zone to merge into competition.

RULE: f.11.2.2
            TITLE: rear impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.2: Rear Impact Protection
c. Other Tractive System components in a position below 350 mm from the ground must be 
protected from rear impact by structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection 
d. Differential mounts or a plate behind upper and lower rear impact tubes may be used as 
Rear Impact Protection.   
If used, the mounts or plate must be 25 mm or more larger at both the top and bottom 
of the upper and lower tubes

RULE: t.1.1.3
            TITLE: fore and aft translation of the template is permitted during insertion.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.1.3: Fore and aft translation of the template is permitted during insertion.
T.1.1.3 Fore and aft translation of the template is permitted during insertion.
